data.title: Pearson Cancels 555,206 Shares Under Ongoing £350m Buyback
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Pearson ( (GB:PSON) ).

Pearson has repurchased 555,206 of its ordinary shares on 1 April 2026 via Morgan Stanley & Co. International at an average price of 985.02 pence, with prices ranging between 973.60 pence and 1,000.00 pence. The acquired shares will be cancelled, reducing the company’s share count and potentially enhancing earnings per share for remaining investors.

This transaction forms part of the second £175 million tranche of Pearson’s £350 million share buyback programme announced earlier in the year. By continuing to execute this sizeable capital return, the group signals confidence in its balance sheet and cash generation, while providing additional support to its share price in the public markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PSON) stock is a Hold with a £10.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pearson stock, see the GB:PSON Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PSON Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PSON is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by steady but not accelerating fundamentals—strong operating profitability offset by low growth, weaker net margin, rising leverage, and softer 2025 free cash flow. Earnings call guidance adds support via clear mid-single-digit growth and profit/cash-conversion targets, while technicals remain a headwind with the stock below key longer-term moving averages and negative MACD. Valuation is reasonable with a mid-teens/high-teens P/E and a ~2.5% dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on PSON stock, click here.

More about Pearson

Pearson plc is a global education and publishing company, best known for its learning materials, assessments and digital education services. Listed on the London Stock Exchange, it focuses on providing educational content and technology to schools, higher education institutions and professional learners worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 2,686,705

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £6.09B

data.title: LSEG Buys Back 339,047 Shares and Updates Voting Rights
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from London Stock Exchange ( (GB:LSEG) ) is now available.

London Stock Exchange Group has continued its share buyback programme with the purchase of 339,047 ordinary shares on 1 April 2026 from Morgan Stanley, paying a volume-weighted average price of 8,839.25p within a range between 8,742.00p and 8,920.00p. The company intends to cancel all of these repurchased shares, signalling an ongoing capital management strategy that returns value to shareholders and may enhance earnings per share.

Following the latest cancellation, LSEG will have 497,274,776 ordinary shares in issue excluding treasury shares and will hold 21,451,599 shares in treasury, setting the company’s total voting rights at 497,274,776. This updated share capital and voting rights figure provides an important reference point for investors and other stakeholders when assessing ownership thresholds and disclosure obligations under UK transparency rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:LSEG) stock is a Buy with a £110.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on London Stock Exchange stock, see the GB:LSEG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LSEG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LSEG is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid underlying financial performance (strong revenue growth and operating margins) and a positive earnings outlook with margin expansion, free-cash-flow targets, and substantial buybacks. These positives are tempered by premium valuation, mixed cash-flow stability in the latest period, and technical signals that suggest short-term strength but less favorable long-term trend positioning.

To see Spark’s full report on LSEG stock, click here.

London Stock Exchange Group plc (LSEG) is a global financial markets infrastructure and data provider, best known for operating the London Stock Exchange and related trading venues. The group offers listing, trading, post-trade, and data and analytics services to issuers, investors, and intermediaries, with a broad international customer base across capital markets.

data.title: Caledonia Mining Issues New Shares and Grants Performance Awards Under Long Term Incentive Plan
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Caledonia Mining ( (CMCL) ).

Caledonia Mining has issued 22,051 new common shares under its long term incentive plan following the vesting of prior awards, increasing its total shares in issue to 19,335,079 with no treasury shares. Admission of the related depositary interests to trading on AIM is expected around April 9, 2026, with CEO Mark Learmonth and CFO Ross Jerrard among the key recipients.

The board’s compensation committee has also approved fresh performance unit awards for senior executives and staff, with vesting tied to delivery on strategic metrics such as construction of the Bilboes Gold Project, production and cost performance at Blanket Mine, and establishing a mineral resource at Motapa. The structure reinforces management alignment with long term project execution and operational performance, though it modestly dilutes existing shareholders as new shares are issued over time.

More about Caledonia Mining

Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc is a gold mining company listed on the NYSE American, AIM and the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange. The group’s key assets include the producing Blanket Mine in Zimbabwe and development projects such as the Bilboes Gold Project and Motapa, giving it a strong operational focus on Southern African gold production and resource growth.

Lloyds Banking ( (GB:LLOY) ) has issued an announcement.

Lloyds Banking Group has assessed the Financial Conduct Authority’s final rules for the industry-wide motor finance redress scheme and currently sees no need to change its existing provision for potential compensation. The bank, however, highlights ongoing uncertainty around customer response rates, operational costs and possible litigation, and plans to update investors with further details alongside its first-quarter results later in April, reiterating its commitment to deliver appropriate and timely redress to affected customers.

data.title: Skanska Sells Remaining Stake in Orlando I-4 Ultimate Project for USD 75 Million
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Skanska AB ( ($SE:SKA.B) ) just unveiled an update.

Skanska has sold its entire 50 percent stake in I-4 Mobility Partners HoldCo LLC, the concession company for the I-4 Ultimate highway project in Orlando, Florida, to its existing partner John Laing I-4 Holdco Corp. The deal, valued at approximately USD 75 million (about SEK 690 million), has closed with necessary approvals and will be booked in Skanska’s Central stream in the second quarter of 2026.

The divestment comes after Skanska led the engineering and construction of the I-4 Ultimate project, which reached substantial completion in February 2022 and previously saw repayment of Skanska’s USD 43 million subordinated debt in 2023. The award‑winning project, which rebuilt and widened 34 kilometres of interstate, has eased congestion and improved safety for more than 200,000 daily vehicles, underscoring Skanska’s capability in large-scale, sustainable transportation infrastructure as it recycles capital from mature assets into new opportunities.

The most recent analyst rating on ($SE:SKA.B) stock is a Buy with a SEK275.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Skanska AB stock, see the SE:SKA.B Stock Forecast page.

More about Skanska AB

Skanska is one of the world’s largest construction and project development companies, with 2025 revenue of SEK 179 billion and around 25,900 employees. It operates across selected markets in the Nordics, Europe and the USA, focusing on innovative and sustainable infrastructure and building solutions that shape how people live, work and connect.

Average Trading Volume: 845,193

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK104B

Learn more about SKA.B stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Spheria Asset Management Ceases to Be Substantial Holder in Praemium
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Praemium Ltd ( (AU:PPS) ) has shared an update.

Spheria Asset Management Pty Ltd has notified Praemium Ltd that it has ceased to be a substantial holder in the company as of 31 March 2026, in line with disclosure requirements under the Corporations Act 2001. The change reflects a reduction in Spheria’s relevant interest and voting power in Praemium shares, which may slightly alter the company’s institutional shareholder mix and could affect perceptions of its register stability among market participants.

No new associates were disclosed in connection with Spheria’s change of holding, and the firm remains listed at its Brisbane address in the filing. The notice underscores ongoing portfolio adjustments by institutional investors in the Australian wealth-platform sector, a space where shifts in substantial holdings are closely watched as indicators of sentiment toward future growth and strategic direction.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:PPS) stock is a Hold with a A$0.77 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Praemium Ltd stock, see the AU:PPS Stock Forecast page.

More about Praemium Ltd

Praemium Ltd is an Australian-listed company that operates in the financial services and technology sector, providing investment platforms and related administration services to advisers, wealth managers and financial institutions. Its core offering focuses on portfolio administration and reporting solutions that support managed accounts and other investment products for clients in Australia and selected international markets.

Average Trading Volume: 1,132,396

data.title: Montana Aerospace lifts margins and cuts debt as pure-play aerospace business
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Montana Aerospace AG ( (CH:AERO) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Montana Aerospace reported a strong 2025 financial year as a focused aerospace player, with net sales from its Aerostructures and Alpine Metal Tech segments rising 15.1% to EUR 979.3 million and adjusted EBITDA up 25.1% to EUR 161.3 million, lifting the margin to 16.5%. The divestments of the E-Mobility and Energy segments, coupled with cost efficiencies and operating leverage, helped sharpen its pure-play profile, improve cash generation, cut net debt to EUR 128.1 million, and underpin guidance for continued growth in sales, profitability and free cash flow through 2027.

Aerostructures remained the core earnings driver, posting 8% sales growth to EUR 881.3 million and a 22.5% jump in adjusted EBITDA as Montana Aerospace benefited from rising OEM demand, capacity expansions in low-cost locations such as Romania and Vietnam, and multi-year contract wins. Alpine Metal Tech more than doubled sales due to a full-year consolidation effect, while disciplined working capital management and liability-to-equity conversions following the Energy divestiture reduced leverage and positioned the group to capitalise on a robust aerospace upcycle despite currency-related hits to the bottom line.

The most recent analyst rating on (CH:AERO) stock is a Buy with a CHF30.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Montana Aerospace AG stock, see the CH:AERO Stock Forecast page.

More about Montana Aerospace AG

Montana Aerospace AG is a Swiss-based supplier of system components and complex assemblies for the aerospace industry, operating 16 engineering and manufacturing sites across four continents. The company employs around 6,300 people and focuses on advanced aluminium, titanium, composite and steel technologies for global OEMs in commercial aerospace and space.

Average Trading Volume: 146,501

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: CHF1.62B
